title: Remember Better: - Highlight Differently: Use Bold Colors or Symbols for Key Points
subtitle: Make Key Info Stand Out
description:
To remember better:
- Highlight differently: Use bold colors or symbols for key points.
- Add uniqueness: Attach a quirky story or image to important details.
- Group by importance: Separate standout items from general information.

Example: If studying a list, underline or use bright markers on the most critical items.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Identify key items to remember and set a target for standout highlights. Aim for 3 to 5 critical points per page or article.

howTo.title: Apply the 3-signal rule
howTo.summary: Choose 3 distinct signals: a bright color for priority items, a symbol for processes, and a quirky anchor for memory. Mark these on your notes.

howTo.title: Perform immediate recall
howTo.summary: After marking, close your notes and attempt to recall the highlighted items aloud. This reinforces memory retention.

howTo.title: Review and adjust
howTo.summary: After 24 hours, revisit your notes and assess recall success. Adjust your highlighting and anchoring strategies as needed.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and refine your memory techniques. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/highlight-recall-assistant/.
